title: Paphiopedilum victoria-regina

This genus is part of the subfamily Cypripedioideae, commonly known as the Venus slipper orchids. intro: Paphiopedilum victoria-regina, commonly known as the Victoria Regia Paphiopedilum or Victoria Queen's Slipper Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the Paphiopedilum genus, characterized by its distinctive slipper-shaped flowers. This orchid has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, headache, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of P. victoria-regina include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which have been shown to possess significant pharmacological activities.
